hypothesis: Knowledge recombination via citation graph missing-edges produces genuine L3+ insights that would not have been discovered through normal session work
method: Run knowledge_recombine.py --cross-only, select candidate [10] (L-1097 x L-950, score=57, shared=L-601,L-908,L-913), attempt manual synthesis of bridge insight

result.novel_insight: Recursion trap (measure-don't-fix attractor) is caused by error-preservation asymmetry operating at meta-knowledge level. Append is cheap, propagation expensive — measurement structurally selected over correction.

finding: Second automated recombination (after L-1129). Produced genuine L4 insight bridging security (error-preservation) and meta (recursion trap). The tool detects gaps that sessions CAN fill but DON'T — because normal dispatch follows domain UCB1, not cross-domain missing-edge topology. Recombination targets the 1575-candidate gap space that domain-internal work cannot reach.
